content.problemStatement:
You are given a string (S)(S)(S). Your task is to determine if the string can be a palindrome after deleting at most one character from it.


Input Format

The first line contains one integer nnn, the size of the string                                                                                                               - Next line contains string SSS.


Output Format

Print whether SSS can be made palindrome after deleting at most one character.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string. The second line contains the string S of length N consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print "true" if the string can become a palindrome after deleting at most one character, otherwise print "false". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Deleting 'b' (or 'c') yields "aca", which is a palindrome.
No single deletion can turn "batr" into a palindrome.
The string is already a palindrome; no deletion needed.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved with a two‑pointer technique. Scan the string from both ends. When the characters match, move both pointers inward. On the first mismatch we have two possibilities: either delete the left character or delete the right character. In each case we check whether the remaining substring is a palindrome by a simple linear scan. If either check succeeds the answer is true, otherwise false. If the whole scan finishes without a mismatch the string is already a palindrome. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (besides the input string)

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static boolean isPalindrome(char[] a,int l,int r){while(l<r){if(a[l]!=a[r])return false;l++;r--;}return true;}private static boolean validPalindrome(char[] a){int l=0,r=a.length-1;while(l<r){if(a[l]==a[r]){l++;r--;}else{return isPalindrome(a,l+1,r)||isPalindrome(a,l,r-1);}}return true;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line;line=br.readLine();while(line!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty())line=br.readLine();int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){line=br.readLine();while(line!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty())line=br.readLine();int n=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());String s=br.readLine();while(s!=null&&s.isEmpty())s=br.readLine();if(s==null)s="";boolean res=validPalindrome(s.toCharArray());out.append(res?"true":"false");if(t+1<T)out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
